input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>false</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>false</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>

data.content: Wait — I used the wrong property. Let me fix that.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>false</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
Wait, I need to double-check this. The correct MSBuild property for Microsoft.Testing.Platform to handle zero tests is not `TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure`. Let me reconsider.

Exit code 8 in Microsoft.Testing.Platform means "no tests were found". The correct way to handle this is to use the `--minimum-expected-tests` command-line option set to 0, or the MSBuild property equivalent.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. In Microsoft.Testing.Platform:
- Exit code 0 = success
- Exit code 8 = no tests found

The way to fix this is by setting `--minimum-expected-tests 0` which tells the platform that finding zero tests is acceptable.


The MSBuild property for this would be via `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` or there might be a dedicated property. Looking at the Microsoft.Testing.Platform documentation, I can pass `--minimum-expected-tests 0` as a command line argument through `dotnet test`, or configure it directly in the project file.

The `--minimum-expected-tests` option defaults to 1, so setting it to 0 treats zero tests as a successful run. The most straightforward approaches are either passing the flag on the command line when running tests, or setting it in the project configuration.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>false</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content: The `--minimum-expected-tests` rejects `0`. But the help output shows `--ignore-exit-code` which is exactly what we need — it can map exit code 8 to 0.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat it as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText: The log says `--minimum-expected-tests` expects a "single non-zero positive integer value". So 0 is not accepted. But there's another option: `--ignore-exit-code 8` which ignores exit code 8 (the "no tests found" exit code) and returns 0 instead. Let me use that approach.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat it as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

     <!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat it as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
     <!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat it as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content:
**The fix:** Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` via the `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` MSBuild property.

Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when no tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag tells the platform to map that specific exit code back to 0, restoring the VSTest behavior for your conditionally-compiled assemblies.

You can also pass this at the command line instead: `dotnet test -- --ignore-exit-code 8`.
